extra.reasoning: The property requires that (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires when elderly AND pregnancy AND low_ejection_fraction. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), which fires when elderly. No rule ever sets either output to false, so conflict resolution and defaults cannot suppress them once enabled. With elderly=true, pregnancy=true, low_ejection_fraction=true: rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true), so both are simultaneously true. In fact, rec_drug_a can only be true when elderly is true, which always forces rec_drug_b true, so the conjunction is unavoidable whenever rec_drug_a holds. The remaining inputs (renal_impairment, bradycardia, diabetes) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set to false. The property is violated.
